title: pyrus scandica var. fennica
intro: Pyrus scandica var. fennica, also known as the wild pear or mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Pyrus scandica var. fennica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
